core_interpretation: Radiation data are exposure/risk and biological-effect context layers; physical dose must be separated from biological injury and modeled long-term risk.
guardrails: Do not infer radiation injury from dose alone.
Do not infer cancer from radiation exposure marker.
Do not infer CNS/cognitive effect without excluding sleep, CO2, stress, workload and medication context.
Do not infer radiation-specific oxidative stress without dose-timing linkage.
Do not treat terrestrial radiation cohorts as identical to space radiation.
research_gaps: Limited astronaut-specific longitudinal biomarker data linking space dosimetry to health outcomes.
Uncertainty in translating terrestrial cohort risks to space environments with different LET/radiation quality.
Need for harmonized protocols integrating omics, cytogenetics, dosimetry and clinical endpoints.
Insufficient data on countermeasure efficacy against chronic low-dose space radiation.
